title: Unified header row — cockpit layout merges date meta with day nav
changes: src/css/report.css — rewrote .header .date-row as a 3-column CSS grid (auto 1fr auto). The previous two-row layout (.date-row above, .day-nav below) is now one row: next-day chip on the right, centered .date-center meta stack, previous-day chip on the left. Removed the standalone .day-nav ruleset (no longer rendered) and replaced with .header .date-row .day-nav-btn scoped selectors that keep unicode-bidi: isolate for bidi-safe Arabic + arrow glyphs. Added @media(max-width:600px) override that collapses the grid to a two-row fallback on narrow screens, pushing .date-center to order:-1 so the meta line stays on top and the nav chips wrap under it.

src/css/report.css — restructured the meta stack into .meta-line1 (day name + Arabic date, larger) and .meta-line2 (hijri · dd/mm/yyyy picker · report counter, smaller/dimmer). The day-nav-current counter is now part of meta-line2 instead of a standalone span, which eliminates the old two-row visual gap between the header and the nav bar.

src/js/app.js — renderReport() no longer builds a flat date-center; it constructs .meta-line1 + .meta-line2 and leaves an empty <span class='day-nav-current'> placeholder for initNav() to fill. This keeps nav.js fully decoupled from the specific DOM of line2.

src/js/ui/nav.js — initNav() now targets .header .date-row instead of creating a sibling .day-nav container. The next-day chip is inserted as the first grid child (right flank in RTL), the previous-day chip is appended as the last grid child (left flank in RTL), and the counter span is populated in place. No new DOM container is created, which means the layout shift between 'header rendered' and 'nav injected' is now limited to the two chips appearing on the outer columns of the existing grid.

src/python/build_index.py — synchronized the embedded initNav() template that regenerates src/js/ui/nav.js on every index rebuild, so future build_index runs don't regenerate the old two-row behavior.

mockups/header-nav-unified.html — kept as a design reference alongside controls-templates.html. Template 05 (Cockpit Arrows) is the one adopted in production; templates 01–04 and 06–07 remain for future header redesigns.
Bumped cache-bust and version-pill strings to 2.5.6 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, and report HTML files.

title: Analytics controls v2 — collapsed filter drawer + segmented grouping
changes: analytics.html — rebuilt the .a-controls row. The six category buttons (بيانات، صفارات، إعلام العدو، إيران، فيديو، حلفاء) are now collapsed behind a single 'فلاتر' drawer button with a live 6/6 badge, and the three grouping buttons (يومي، أسبوعي، شهري) became a true segmented control with a sliding golden thumb. The .a-controls class was kept as-is so mountExcelButton('.a-controls') still attaches the Excel action to the far end of the row without any change to excel-button.js.

src/js/analytics/controls.js — replaced the old per-button listeners with initDrawer() and initSegmentedGroup(). The state contract is identical: _state.categories is still a mutating array of the same six keys and _state.grouping is still 'daily'/'weekly'/'monthly', so buildWhere() and getGroupFormat() keep working unchanged. Drawer supports outside-click and Escape to close, tracks a 'partial' state when 1 ≤ checked < 6 (button flips to gold border), and the segmented thumb is positioned in RTL via getBoundingClientRect() right-offset math so it slides correctly under direction:rtl without CSS translate fiddling.

src/css/analytics.css — appended the .a-controls-v2, .a-drawer-*, and .a-seg-* component styles. All colors flow through existing CSS variables (--surface2, --accent, --border, --text-dim), so the light theme automatically inherits correct contrast; explicit body.analytics.light overrides added only for the drawer panel background and segmented-button active text color where the variable palette needs inversion. Old .a-ctrl / .a-ctrl-sep rules are left in place for backward compatibility but are no longer referenced by analytics.html.

mockups/controls-templates.html — added an interactive side-by-side preview of all five control-row design templates that drove the decision to adopt the drawer pattern. Kept in the repo as a design reference and as a test bed for future control-row iterations.
Bumped cache-bust and version pill strings to 2.5.5 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, report, and src/js/app.js.

title: Remove site-wide PDF export, keep analytics Excel export
changes: Deleted src/js/export/pdf-button.js and every call site that mounted it. The print-to-PDF button was cluttering report.html, timeline.html, and all 31 monthly chronicle pages with a duplicate of what the browser's native Ctrl+P already does.

Stripped the mountPdfButton() <script> block from report.html, timeline.html, and all monthly/*.html files. Also removed the same block from the build_monthly.py template so future chronicle rebuilds stay clean.

Kept the analytics Excel export (src/js/export/excel-button.js + build-workbook.js) untouched — that one produces a real 6-sheet .xlsx artifact with KPIs, events over time, hourly density, top targets, and raw data, which the browser cannot replicate.
Left src/css/print.css intact: it is still linked as media="print" on every page, so manual browser printing still gets proper styling. It is not an export button, just a print stylesheet.
Bumped version-pill strings to 2.5.4 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, report, and app.js.

title: Fix bidi-mangled day navigation bar
changes: src/css/report.css — added a real .day-nav flex container with gap, border, and proper styling. Before: the day-nav div had NO CSS rule at all, and the .header .day-nav-btn rules never matched because day-nav is injected as a sibling of .header (not a child), so the three nav elements collapsed into a single unstyled inline run.

Bidi fix: in RTL context, the unstyled inline flow let the LTR counter ('917 / 918') visually fuse with the Arabic date text in the adjacent button, producing garbage like '→ 11 نيسان917 / 9189 نيسان ←'. Added unicode-bidi: isolate on every .day-nav-btn and reached the .day-nav-current span (previously only scoped under .date-center) so numbers render as their own isolated run.
Hover and .disabled selectors extended to match buttons inside .day-nav, not just under .header — disabled end-of-range buttons now correctly fade to 0.3 opacity, and hover state actually triggers.

title: Simpler final CTA — button only
changes: index.html — removed the 'جاهز؟' heading and the 'أنت الآن تعرف كلّ ما تحتاجه...' lead paragraph from the .final-cta block at the bottom of the landing page. The primary 'ابدأ التصفّح' button and the version pill stay exactly where they were.

Motivation: by the time a reader reaches the final CTA they have already seen the walkthrough — the heading + copy were restating what the whole page above already teaches, so dropping them makes the call-to-action more direct and reduces visual noise on the closing screen.
No CSS changes: .final-cta already centers its children, so the button sits naturally below the FAQ without touching layout styles.

title: Hover tooltip on hours-distribution graphs
changes: src/js/dashboards/helpers.js — buildTimeline now wires every .cat-tbar to a lazy-created shared .cat-tbar-tooltip element via mouseenter/mouseleave + focus/blur handlers. Replaces the slow native bar.title attribute that took ~1s to appear and had no styling.

Tooltip content: HH:00 → HH:00 in LTR monospace plus an Arabic count line that pluralizes (حدث / أحداث). Built with DOM nodes — no innerHTML — so it stays XSS-safe even if data ever gets less trusted.
Smart positioning: tooltip is anchored above the bar, but flips below when there is no room above, and clamps horizontally to the viewport so edge bars never push it offscreen.
Bars now have tabIndex=0 and a focus-visible ring, so keyboard users get the same hover treatment as mouse users — fixes a small accessibility gap on every dashboard.
src/css/enhancements.css — added .cat-tbar-tooltip styles with dark + light theme variants, fade-in animation, RTL count row + LTR monospace time row.
All four hour graphs (bayanat, sirens, enemy, iran dashboards) inherit the new tooltip from a single helper change — no per-dashboard edits needed.

title: Finance-grade interactive line chart on the analytics dashboard
changes: Replaced the hand-rolled SVG line-chart renderer (~200 LOC) with Apache ECharts 5.5.1, served from jsDelivr and only loaded on analytics.html. The renderer keeps its old signature renderLineChart(id, datasets, height, {xLabels, grouping}), so dashboard.js and every refresh path (date range, category toggle, daily/weekly/monthly) work unchanged.

Stock-terminal interactions now available on the 'الأحداث بمرور الوقت' panel: mouse wheel zooms in/out on the time axis, the bottom range-slider pans and sets the visible window, the toolbox magnifier rect-zooms by drag, the brush tool highlights an X-range, the restore button resets, and saveAsImage exports a 2x-DPI PNG named harbi-analytics.png.

Crosshair tooltip with all series values at the cursor position, RTL formatting, theme-aware colors read from CSS vars (--accent, --border, --text-dim, --surface) so future light-theme work just inherits.

Layout: group badge (يومي/أسبوعي/شهري) anchored top-left, toolbox icons top-right, legend on its own centered row below the toolbox so the two never collide. Container height bumped to fit the slider and two header rows; ResizeObserver keeps the canvas in sync with fullscreen toggling.
Bumped all version-pill strings to 2.5.0 across the 6 user-facing HTML pages and app.js. Cache-bust query params auto-update via the existing string replace.

title: Remove multi-column sort from analytics data table
changes: Reverted the analytics 'Most Active Days' table to single-column sorting per user request — multi-sort, the sort-chain chip bar, and the priority badges added in v2.3.7/v2.3.8 are all gone

src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — restored single _tableSortKey + _tableSortDir state, removed buildOrderBy() and the multi-sort onSort handler, removed onSortRemove. ORDER BY is now built inline as 'key dir, date DESC' (date stays as a stable tiebreaker for pagination determinism)
src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderTable now takes opts.sortKey/opts.sortDir only, no opts.sort array, no chip bar DOM, no sortIndex/showPriority logic. Header click no longer forwards shiftKey
src/css/analytics.css — already had the .a-sort-bar/.a-sort-chip*/.a-sort-badge/th.sorted blocks removed in an earlier edit; verified no remnants
Kept the column-key whitelist in dashboard.js (_sortableKeys) — it's still useful as a defense-in-depth guard around the SQL ORDER BY string concat, even with single-sort
Kept the cache-bust ?v= query strings on every analytics asset import from v2.3.9 — those are still useful for forcing browsers to refetch on every release

title: Cache-bust analytics module imports so multi-sort actually loads
changes: Root cause diagnosis: analytics multi-sort was reported as 'still not working' even after v2.3.7 and v2.3.8. A fresh headless-browser test on the same commit confirmed the SQL sort WAS working (4 successive click tests produced 4 distinct row orders) — the user's browser was running a stale cached copy of dashboard.js / charts.js from before the multi-sort refactor.

analytics.html — added ?v=2.3.8 cache-bust query params to every local JS and CSS import (dashboard.js, spotlight.js, enhancements.css, analytics.css, navbar.css, spotlight.css, print.css). ES modules in Chrome are aggressively cached and stay stale until the URL changes.

Since my Python bumper already auto-replaces '?v=<old>' → '?v=<new>' on every version bump, wiring the query params with the current version now means all future analytics asset changes will cache-bust automatically on each release, no more manual intervention needed.
Bumped all version-pill strings to 2.3.9 across the 6 user-facing HTML pages and app.js.

title: Visible sort-chain status bar for analytics table
changes: Fixed perceived 'sort doesn't work' issue: the underlying SQL multi-sort was correct (verified in a headless browser — rows tying on the primary key were correctly ordered by the secondary key), but the UX was invisible when primary-key values were unique. The secondary sort had no visible effect, so users thought nothing happened.

Added a sort-chain status bar above the data table — renders one accent-colored chip per active sort column, each showing its priority rank, the column label, a direction toggle button (▲/▼), and a × remove button. This makes the multi-sort state always visible even when primary-unique values mask the tiebreaker.
Chip direction button reuses the existing onSort(key, shift=true) path to preserve the column's position in the chain while flipping its direction.
Chip × button routes through a new onSortRemove(key) callback in dashboard.js, which filters the key out of _tableSort and falls back to the default [{total, desc}] sort if the chain becomes empty.
Bigger, more prominent header priority badges (22px circle, 0.85rem bold) — previously 18px/0.7rem and barely visible; direction arrow on sorted headers bumped from 1rem to 1.2rem 900-weight.
Added a subtle highlight on sorted column headers: .sorted gets --accent-dim background; .sorted-primary also gets an inset 3px --accent underline so the primary sort is unambiguous at a glance.
Added an Arabic hint ('Shift+نقرة لفرز متعدد') at the left edge of the sort bar so first-time users discover the multi-sort interaction.
Fullscreen scales: sort bar, chips, and priority badges all bump up (1.1rem fonts, 26px chip widgets) so they stay legible when the panel fills the viewport.

title: Multi-column sort on the analytics data table
changes: Analytics 'Most Active Days' table now supports multi-column sorting — click a header to replace the current sort, Shift+click to add it as a secondary/tertiary sort or toggle its direction if already in the list
Each active sort column shows its arrow and, when more than one column is sorted, a numeric priority badge (1/2/3...) so it's clear which column drives the primary order
ORDER BY is now built from the full sort chain with an implicit 'date DESC' tiebreaker so pagination stays stable even when every active sort key ties

src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — replaced single _tableSortKey/_tableSortDir state with a _tableSort array of {key,dir}. Added a _sortableKeys whitelist to guard the SQL ORDER BY string concat from any stray key names

src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderTable now accepts opts.sort (multi-sort array), still honors the legacy opts.sortKey/opts.sortDir for backward compatibility, and forwards ev.shiftKey to the onSort callback
Added an Arabic title tooltip on the table explaining the single-click vs Shift+click interaction model

title: Larger analytics data table — second pass
changes: Further bumped .a-table font-size from 0.92rem → 1.2rem normal and 1.1rem → 1.5rem in fullscreen; header 0.78rem → 1rem normal and 0.92rem → 1.2rem in fullscreen
Widened cell padding to match the larger type: 16px/18px normal, 20px/24px in fullscreen so rows don't feel cramped at the new scale
Sort-arrow indicator bumped 0.8rem → 1rem with 6px margin for visual balance against the bigger header labels
Pager buttons bumped to 1.05rem with 10px/22px padding and 8px radius; pager info also 1.05rem to stay consistent with the new table scale

title: Bigger, more readable analytics data table
changes: src/css/analytics.css — .a-table font-size bumped from 0.64rem → 0.92rem body / 0.78rem header, with wider 12px/14px cell padding; fullscreen mode goes further to 1.1rem body / 0.92rem header with 16px/18px padding
src/js/analytics/charts.js — removed the inline font-size:0.6rem override on date cells that was defeating the CSS rule; date cells now inherit the table font-size and keep their monospace styling
Bumped the inline sort-arrow indicator from 0.5rem → 0.8rem with a small margin so it reads correctly next to the new larger header labels
src/css/analytics.css — pager buttons and info text bumped from ~0.6rem → 0.85rem with roomier 8px/18px padding so pagination controls stay visually consistent with the bigger table

title: Bigger, more visible line-chart axis labels
changes: src/js/analytics/charts.js — line-chart Y-axis labels bumped from 8px → 14px and X-axis labels from 7.5px → 13px, with color moved from --text-dim to --text and font-weight 600 so they actually read clearly in both normal and fullscreen mode
Widened axis margins: marginL 40→56 and marginB 24→38 so the bigger labels have breathing room and don't collide with the plot area or get clipped
Tightened label offsets (Y: marginL-4→marginL-8 and +3→+5 vertical nudge; X: h-4→h-10) so the larger glyphs sit where the eye expects them

Removed preserveAspectRatio='none' from the SVG — it was causing the new larger glyphs to stretch horizontally in fullscreen since the container aspect ratio differs from the viewBox. The viewBox is already sized to match the container so uniform scaling is the correct default

Replaced the pixel-threshold fullscreen heuristic in renderLineChart with a deterministic closest('.a-panel-fs') check — prevents a re-draw feedback loop where legend toggles could otherwise keep nudging the measured height above a static threshold

title: Analytics panel fullscreen actually fills the viewport
changes: Fixed: entering fullscreen on an analytics panel left the chart at its normal ~220px height with the rest of the screen empty — the line chart's SVG was being rendered at a caller-fixed pixel height, so growing the container did nothing
src/css/analytics.css — .a-panel-fs is now a flex column; .a-line-chart/#heatmapPanel/#barChart/#dataTable get flex:1 1 auto + min-height:0 so they claim all remaining viewport height after the title

src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderLineChart now measures el.clientHeight and, when the container is taller than the caller's base height, re-derives the SVG dimensions to fill it (preserveAspectRatio=none keeps the axes crisp end-to-end)
src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — entering/exiting fullscreen on a chart panel now triggers refresh() inside a requestAnimationFrame so charts re-measure and re-render at the new container size
Bonus: fullscreen bar chart now shows taller bars, larger labels, and 10px gap between rows so it actually uses the space too; heatmap gets a wider gap + larger hour labels

title: Fix site-wide light theme consistency (index, spotlight, analytics)
changes: Fixed the broken inline <head> theme-init script on every page: it called document.body.classList.add('light') while document.body was still null, throwing a silent TypeError so the saved 'light' class never applied on first paint

Replaced the one-liner with a robust IIFE that sets the class on <html> synchronously (pre-FOUC) then on <body> via a DOMContentLoaded fallback — patched across 35 HTML files (index, archive, report, timeline, and all 32 monthly/*.html pages)
Added the missing inline theme script to analytics.html, which previously had none and always rendered dark regardless of user preference

Made initNavbar() in src/js/ui/navbar.js authoritative: it now re-applies the saved theme to <html> and <body> on every page load, guaranteeing a single source of truth even if an inline script ever regresses
index.html inline <style> now defines a body.light { ... } palette block matching the site-wide light tokens — the landing page previously had no light overrides at all, so it was stuck dark
src/css/index.css now ships a body.light override block, which also fixes every monthly/*.html chronicle page (they share this stylesheet)
src/css/spotlight.css now has a body.light fork for all --sl-* variables plus softer overlay dim and modal shadow — the Spotlight search overlay was hard-coded dark and ignored the active theme
Also hardened src/js/ui/theme.js initTheme() the same null-body-safe way to stay consistent with initNavbar
Bumped footer version pills site-wide to v2.3.2, including two stale v2.2.14 pills in index.html that were missed by the 2.3.1 sync

title: Site-wide footer trailer + version pill sync
changes: Added the source-attribution / non-official disclaimer / version-pill footer to the tutorial landing page (index.html), which previously shipped without it — now every user-facing page carries the same trailer
Scoped the new landing-page footer as .site-footer (inlined CSS) since index.html does not link src/css/index.css — avoids any collision with the shared .footer rule used elsewhere
Ported the full showChangelog() overlay + data-disclaimer-slot markup into index.html so the version pill opens the changelog modal consistently across the site
Synced stale version pills: archive.html (v2.2.13), index.html final-cta CTA pill (v2.2.14), and the runtime-rendered footer in src/js/app.js used by report.html (v2.2.14) now all read v2.3.1
Unified the disclaimer.js?v= cache-bust query param on archive.html (v2.2.11 → v2.3.1) so it no longer lags behind every other page

title: Export reports as PDF (editorial pages) + Excel (analytics)
changes: Added download button on report.html, timeline.html, and all monthly chronicle pages — one click triggers a native PDF via window.print() with a dedicated @media print stylesheet
New src/css/print.css: hides navbar/spotlight/buttons, preserves RTL Arabic, forces cards to stay whole across pages, and makes monthly chronicles break one day per page for archival use

Added Excel export on analytics.html — lazy-loads SheetJS (~400KB) only on first click, then builds a 6-sheet workbook (KPIs, Events Over Time, Hourly Density, Most Active Days, Top Targets, Raw Data) by running the same queries the dashboard uses
New src/js/export/: pdf-button.js mounts the shared button, build-workbook.js is a pure testable function (14 browser assertions), excel-button.js wires the analytics click-to-download flow
Zero new runtime dependencies on the critical path — SheetJS loads only when someone actually clicks export

Added site favicon (favicon.svg at project root): dark navy rounded square with gold crosshair target ring and Arabic letter ح centered, matching the existing #080c12/#c9a84c dossier palette; wired into index.html, report.html, archive.html, analytics.html, timeline.html, test-db.html, and the monthly template in src/python/build_monthly.py

title: Tutorial-style landing page + calendar moved to Archive button
changes: New index.html is a 5-step guided walkthrough: open calendar, pick a day, read a report, search, view map, export — each step illustrated with a fake browser window mockup
Calendar app (the previous index.html) moved to archive.html (git mv preserves history)
Added new 'الأرشيف' nav item in src/js/ui/navbar.js pointing to archive.html, with active-page detection for the archive route
Landing page includes 6-question FAQ with native <details>/<summary> expand/collapse, no JavaScript required
Added 6 landing-page mockup explorations in mockups/ (editorial, anatomy, dashboard, manifesto, walkthrough, quickstart) as design-direction reference

title: Maps now render real city borders with info drawer + POLY/DOT toggle
changes: Replaced circle-marker dots on all three maps (sirenMap, autoSirenMap, autoBayanMap) with real OSM admin polygons where available, synthesized polygons otherwise
Pre-baked 134 place borders via new src/python/fetch_borders.py (47 real OSM polygons from Nominatim, 87 synthetic fallbacks) into data/borders.json
Added slide-in info drawer per map (4th map button ≡) with intensity legend + click-to-zoom location list, following Option B from the panel-layouts prototype
Added POLY/DOT mode toggle (5th map button) so viewers can flip between border and legacy dot rendering on demand
Extracted all polygon/dot/synthesis/drawer logic into shared src/js/maps/border-renderer.js so future maps inherit the same UX
Color ramp by intensity: green 1, yellow 2–3, orange 4–6, red 7+

title: Satellite view toggle on siren and bayanat maps
changes: Added SAT/MAP toggle button below the fullscreen control on all Leaflet maps
Uses ESRI World Imagery satellite tiles (zoom up to 18) vs Carto dark/light street tiles
Theme swap (light/dark) now respects satellite mode and keeps imagery when toggled
Wired into siren-map, siren-auto-map and bayanat-map via shared satellite-toggle module

title: Expanded sheet now covers search row and filters (true full-screen)
changes: Moved sheet's positioning context from .sl-split to .sl-modal so it can extend above the top bars
Expanded state now fills 100% of the modal (was constrained to the split area below the filters)
Top bars (search row, info bar, filters) pinned with z-index 11 so they remain above the dim backdrop and usable in collapsed state
Sheet z-index raised to 20 so it always sits above the top bars when expanded
Drag-height cap now reads from the modal rather than the split (correct containing block)

title: Fix: tapping a result no longer instantly closes the mobile sheet
changes: Result tap was opening the sheet and then the same bubbling click was triggering the split's tap-outside-to-close handler — both happening in a single event cycle, so the sheet never stayed open
Split click handler now bails when e.target is inside .sl-results (not just .sl-detail), letting result clicks pass through without being treated as outside-taps

title: Spotlight bottom sheet: swipe-up expand + tap-outside close
changes: Swipe up on the drag handle now expands the sheet from 68vh to 95vh full-height state
Swipe down from expanded collapses back to 68vh; another swipe down closes it
Tapping the dimmed backdrop (outside the sheet) now closes the preview
Drag handler rewritten as a state machine (collapsed ↔ expanded → closed)
Sheet height animates smoothly with a cubic-bezier transition; corners un-round slightly when expanded for a more immersive feel

title: Spotlight mobile: bottom-sheet preview with drag-to-dismiss
changes: Mobile result preview is now a 68vh bottom sheet — list peek remains visible behind the drawer
Added drag handle at top of sheet with swipe-down to dismiss (threshold: 80px)
Tapping the drag handle also closes the sheet; dimmed backdrop over the list peek
Replaced the full-screen mobile detail view (v2.2.7) with the bottom-sheet pattern
New search query automatically closes the sheet so results regain focus
Back button removed on mobile — drag handle is the new close affordance

title: Mobile spotlight: header search icon always visible + mobile preview drawer
changes: Header: search icon now always visible on mobile as an icon-only button (was hidden behind hamburger)
Spotlight: tapping a result on mobile now opens a full-screen preview drawer with a back button
Spotlight mobile: reduced padding, readable font sizes, sticky accent 'open report' button, slide-up animation
Spotlight mobile: search input font-size raised to 16px to prevent iOS zoom-on-focus
Spotlight mobile: footer keyboard hints hidden (desktop-only), full-height 100vh modal

title: Deep timeline fact-check: 10 corrections across all 6 chapters
changes: Ch1 Oct 7 rocket count: 3,000+ 'in the first hour' → 2,200+/آلاف in opening wave (closer to IDF/Hamas reconciled figures)
Ch2 Al-Shifa hospital raid date: 17 Nov → 15 Nov 2023 (the actual first IDF ground entry)
Ch2 Gaza 40k toll: 'by September' → 'by August' (crossed Aug 15, 2024)
Ch2 Nuseirat operation toll: 200+ → 274 per Gaza Health Ministry (narrative + step + event card)
Ch3 Major assassinations stat: 4 → 5 (Arouri + Zahedi + Shukr + Haniyeh + Deif)
Ch4 Lebanon martyrs stat: 3,700+ → ~4,000 (closer to final MoH ceasefire count of ~3,961)
Ch5 Assad regime duration: 53 years → 54 years (Nov 1970 → Dec 2024) — 4 locations
Ch5 ceasefire violations: 100+ → 1,500+ (more accurate for the period through early 2025)
Ch6 Gaza total: 50,000+ → 60,000+ (was stale, reflecting early 2025 figure)
Ch6 war days total: 550+ → 920+ (was stale by ~370 days)

title: Fact-fix: Naim Qassem elected 29 Oct 2024, not 2025
changes: Timeline Chapter 4 (Sep-Nov 2024): added Qassem's election on 29 Oct 2024 to narrative, step-by-step, event strip, and tags
Timeline Chapter 5 (Dec 2024-Mar 2025): removed 3 wrong mentions placing Qassem's election in Jan/Feb 2025
Qassem was elected the day before Halloween 2024, one month after Nasrallah's assassination — belongs in the war chapter, not post-ceasefire

title: Fix: hijri date missing on 61% of report pages
changes: 565 of 919 data files had empty hijri — header showed an orphan separator and gap
Now computes hijri on the fly via Intl.DateTimeFormat(islamic-umalqura) when data.hijri is empty
Stored hijri values take priority, so dates you already have keep their original text
Hijri span + its leading separator are hidden entirely if both stored and computed fail (very old browsers)

title: Ultra-compact report header (template D)
changes: Report header row redesigned as a single compact line: day • dateAr • hijri • picker • counter, with prev/next pinned to edges
Date picker trigger is now a dotted-underline accent link (monospace) instead of a boxy chip
Counter uses tabular-nums so digits don't jitter on nav
Hijri shrunk to 0.72rem muted; main date text is 0.82rem; day name is gold

title: Navbar brand, monthly default, merged report header row
changes: Navbar brand is now «★ أرشيف الإعلام الحربي» (was «★ الإعلام الحربي»)
Monthly chronicle default link points to 2023-10 (war chronicle starts from October 2023)
Report header: prev/next buttons and day counter merged into the same row as the date and date picker — one unified styled row with accent separator

title: Unified footer disclaimer notice across all pages
changes: All pages (index, report, timeline, analytics, monthly) now show the same full disclaimer notice: non-affiliation covers affiliated entities, AI processing explicitly noted as error-prone
analytics.html and timeline.html footers aligned with index layout

title: Legal disclaimer modal across all pages
changes: New 7-point disclaimer modal: non-affiliation, data source, AI processing, neutrality, content liability, copyright/fair use, no warranties
Disclaimer link added to footers on index, report, timeline, analytics, and monthly chronicle pages
analytics.html now has a footer (was missing)
Shared src/js/ui/disclaimer.js auto-injects link into any [data-disclaimer-slot] element

title: Monthly chronicle pages
changes: Auto-generated War Chronicle page for each month (31 pages)
Narrative brief, turning-point cards, scroll effects, counter animations
Prev/next navigation between months
Linked from index calendar view

title: Heatmap tooltip time range uses arrow
changes: Time range in tooltip now shows 09:00 → 10:00 instead of 09:00 – 10:00

title: Clearer heatmap tooltip labels
changes: Intensity bar labeled 'الحدّة: 85% من الذروة' instead of bare percentage
Day total labeled 'كل أيام الخميس: 244 حدث' instead of 'إجمالي الخميس'
Hour total labeled 'كل الأيام الساعة 10:00: 81 حدث' on its own line

title: Add barija/warship/battleship/ship/naval aliases
changes: Add barija, warship, battleship, ship, naval to alias dictionary

title: Heatmap labels aligned in fullscreen + rich tooltip
changes: Day labels use CSS grid matching heatmap rows — aligned perfectly in normal and fullscreen
Rich tooltip: time range (15:00–16:00), event count with % of total, intensity bar, day and hour totals

title: Analytics heatmap full day names + date range arrow layout
changes: Heatmap day labels: full Arabic names (الأحد, الإثنين, ...) with fixed-width alignment
Date range: replaced من/إلى labels with fromDate → toDate arrow layout

title: Fix analytics default to daily granularity
changes: Fix daily button not pre-selected — moved HTML 'on' class from monthly to daily

title: Analytics default grouping set to daily
changes: Analytics dashboard now defaults to daily granularity instead of monthly

title: Spotlight polish — suggestions, date format, filter pills
changes: Replace search history with shuffled suggestions (8 random from 28 curated entries)
Detail pane date now shows Arabic month name + small DD/MM/YYYY hint
Fix spotlight stuck on loading — debounce now awaits DB ready
Polished filter pills with elevated background and count badges
Trailer version tag updated to match

title: Smart Spotlight Search — complete rewrite with split-pane UI
changes: Split-pane spotlight (Ctrl+K): results list + live detail preview like macOS Spotlight
310-entry English→Arabic alias dictionary (weapons, locations, groups, military terms)
Keyboard layout transliteration: forgot to switch keyboard? Type English keys, get Arabic
Phonetic transliteration: type 'haifa' and find حيفا
Natural language date search: 'today', 'yesterday', 'Oct 7 2024', '3 days ago', 'last week'
Date + keyword combo: '2024-10-07 drone' filters results to that day
Category-specific detail pane: badge/weapon/tags for bayanat, location for sirens, source for iran
Search term highlighting in results and full text preview
Related events: see 3 nearby events from the same day
Search history persisted in localStorage (last 10 searches)
Stats bar shows live per-category result counts as you type
EN→AR mode indicator shows when transliteration is active
Tab key cycles through category filters
Cmd+Enter opens result in new tab
Loading skeleton while database initializes
SQLite FTS5 only — faster, more consistent search
Mobile responsive: full-screen on small screens
Spotlight now available on analytics page

title: Fix phase nav visibility — proper z-index and offset
changes: Phase nav z-index raised to 199 (just below main navbar at 200)
Top offset corrected to 58px (navbar 56px + 2px border)
Gold accent bottom border for clear visual separation
Stronger shadow for depth

title: Timeline phase nav — prominent sticky bar with scroll tracking
changes: Phase nav background changed to surface color with shadow + border
Larger padding and font sizes for better visibility
Active item highlighted with bold text and surface2 background
Glowing progress fill bars track scroll position per chapter

title: Fix timeline phase nav — visible scroll-tracking progress bars
changes: Fix phase nav hidden behind main navbar (sticky top offset)
Add glowing per-chapter progress fill bars that track scroll position
Completed chapters show dimmed fill, active chapter shows live glow
Enriched all 6 chapters with comprehensive data and events

title: New Timeline page — scroll-driven war chronicle
changes: Add timeline.html with 6 chapters covering Oct 2023 to present
Scroll-driven storytelling: hero, sticky phase navigator, reveal animations
Horizontal event strips + vertical step-by-step breakdowns per chapter
Scroll progress bar, hover effects, staggered reveals
Full dark/light theme support and mobile responsive
Add timeline link to navbar between home and analytics

title: Analytics defaults to current month
changes: Default date range is now current month instead of entire dataset
Date pickers still allow navigating the full range

title: Full data consistency sweep — fix 918 days
changes: Fix 43 bayan spillovers (late-night next-day posts moved to correct files)
Fix 36 stats mismatches (stats synced to actual array lengths)
Fix 12 wrong day names (dayAr) and date formats (dateAr)
Generate 85 missing siren map points across 68 days
Expand geocoding dictionary with 25 new locations (Gaza, Eilat, southern)
Create 2026-04-11.json for 3 spillover bayans from Apr 10
Add validate_data.py (12-check validator) and fix_data.py (auto-fixer)
Rebuild all indexes: reports-meta, spotlight, nav, harbi.db

title: Update report for Apr 10 — full day (155 messages)
changes: Re-fetch Apr 10 with full day coverage (was mid-day only)
62 bayanat, 38 sirens, 21 enemy media, 2 Iran, 4 videos

title: Clean up .gitignore — ignore generated artifacts
changes: Ignore raw/ directory (772 JSON data files)
Ignore tmp/ directory (generated report PNGs/HTMLs)
Ignore report-hero and test-db.html one-off test files
Remove redundant JSDoc comment from db.js

title: Fix tab badge counts on all 917 reports
changes: Fix JS to read full stat key names (bayanat/sirens/enemy/iran/videos/allies) matching JSON data
Normalize 9 short-key JSON files to consistent full key format
Rebuild database with correct counts

title: Inline header dates on same line
changes: Place Arabic date and Gregorian dd/mm/yyyy picker on the same row using flex layout

title: Fix changelog versions and footer version sync
changes: Align all changelog versions with actual git tags (1.7.6–1.8.2)
Add timestamps to changelog dates
Fix footer version to match latest deploy

title: Fix report counter order — swap values for RTL rendering
changes: Swap counter values so RTL bidi renders as current/total (e.g. 909/917)

title: Fix report counter display order in RTL
changes: Add direction:ltr to day-nav-current so 910/917 reads left-to-right correctly

title: Clean up report header: remove title, fix badges, compact search
changes: Remove redundant h1 headline from report header
Fix tab badge counts using correct JSON stat keys (b/s/e/ir/v/al)
Move search bar into tabs row as a compact inline input

title: Fix datepicker trigger not clickable due to header overlay
changes: Add position:relative and z-index:1 to datepicker trigger so it sits above header::before overlay

title: Add datepicker to report header for quick date navigation
changes: Add clickable dd/mm/yyyy below the Arabic date in report header
Reuse analytics datepicker with calendar popup, manual input, and today button
Fix datepicker trigger showing Arabic format — restore dd/mm/yyyy after init

title: Style MM/YYYY as subtle subtitle under month name
changes: Move numeric date (MM/YYYY) to a smaller styled line below the Arabic month name
Muted gray color with letter-spacing for clean look

title: Add 27 missing Feb 2026 reports — zero gaps from Oct 7 2023
changes: Add 27 reports for Feb 1-27, 2026 (ceasefire period, mostly enemy media and Iran news)
Complete coverage: 917 consecutive days from Oct 7 2023 to Apr 10 2026 with no gaps
Rebuild all indexes (reports-meta, spotlight, nav, harbi.db)

title: Fix: restore theme toggle, dashboards, maps, and nav on all reports
changes: Fix missing initNav export that silently broke all enhancements (theme, dashboards, maps)
Add prev/next day navigation bar to all reports
Wrap each enhancement init in try-catch so one failure never cascades
Add .catch() to dynamic import for visible error logging

title: Add 732 reports: Feb 2024 - Jan 2026
changes: Add 213 reports for Feb-Aug 2024 (Lebanon border front: 1,609 bayanat, 1,617 sirens)
Add 153 reports for Sep 2024-Jan 2025 (ground war peak: Oct 929 sirens, Nov ceasefire)
Add 366 reports for Feb 2025-Jan 2026 (ceasefire period, Jun 2025 Iran escalation)
Archive now spans 890 reports from Oct 7, 2023 to Apr 10, 2026
Add batch_split.py utility for efficient multi-day report generation

title: Performance: pre-built search index + externalized data
changes: Spotlight search loads 1 pre-built index instead of 159 fetches (~450KB gzipped vs ~7.8MB)
Externalize reports/stats/searchIndex from index.html to cached reports-meta.js (81KB → 25KB)
Add build_index.py — auto-generates all derived indexes from data/*.json
categorize.py now auto-calls build_index.py — no manual index edits needed

title: Add reports for Nov 2023 - Jan 2024 (92 days)
changes: Add 92 historical reports covering Nov 1, 2023 through Jan 31, 2024
6,669 messages total — ceasefire visible Nov 24-30 (low activity)
Hezbollah bayanat peak at 22/day (Nov 23) before temporary truce

title: Add reports for Oct 15-31, 2023 (Toufan Al-Aqsa weeks 2-3)
changes: Add 17 historical reports covering Oct 15-31, 2023
Total: 1,243 messages — Hezbollah operations ramp up from Oct 20 onward
Bayanat begin appearing regularly as resistance operations formalize

title: Add reports for Oct 7-14, 2023 (Toufan Al-Aqsa week 1)
changes: Add 8 historical reports covering the first week of Toufan Al-Aqsa (Oct 7-14, 2023)
Total: 737 messages across 8 days — 148 messages on Oct 7 alone
Mostly enemy media coverage and siren alerts from the opening days of the war

title: Add report for Apr 10 + light theme default
changes: Add report for 2026-04-10: 32 bayanat, 13 sirens, 11 enemy media, 3 videos
Light theme is now the default for new visitors (dark still available via toggle)

title: Fix first siren card appearing above dashboard
changes: Fix siren dashboard insertion: anchor to sirenMap element instead of first card
Dashboard, filters, and list title now insert between map and first card

title: Fix sirens: map dots, missing fullText for Apr 1-7
changes: Add missing sirenPoints to 5 data files (Apr 2-6) — map dots now render
Add missing fullText to sirens in 4 data files (Apr 1,3,4,7) — toggle button now shows
Computed sirenPoints from location names using SIREN_COORDS lookup

title: Fix global search navigating to wrong card
changes: Fix search results landing on wrong card due to bayanat phase reordering
Add data-src-idx attribute to all cards for index-safe deep-linking
Fix competing smooth scrolls on mobile
Add in-place navigation for same-report spotlight results

title: Full codebase restructure into ES modules
changes: Split 3 monolithic JS files (2623 lines) into 22 focused ES modules under src/js/
Organized into src/js/renderers/, dashboards/, maps/, ui/, search/
Extracted inline CSS from index.html to src/css/index.css
Moved CSS to src/css/, Python to src/python/
Deleted 8 legacy report HTML files (~1.2MB) superseded by SPA
Replaced switchTab monkey-patching with clean onTabSwitch hook pattern
Added ESLint, Prettier, Ruff tooling configs
Fixed pre-push hook to use new src/js/app.js path

title: Regenerate Apr 8 report with full categorization
changes: Override-regenerated 2026-04-08 — categorizer was missing Hezbollah communiques (showing b:0 for a day with 2 major communiques about ceasefire and civilian massacres)
categorize.py: recognize 'بيان صادر عن حزب الله' as bayanat with badge='communique' and content-derived target
categorize.py: extend Iran filter to catch 'الحرس الثوري' / '#إيران' / 'الوعد الصادق' (caught Iranian Wa'd Sadiq statement #56)
categorize.py: extend Iraq filter to catch 'حركة النجباء' / 'أكرم الكعبي' / '#العراق' / 'الاسلامية' spelling variant (caught Najaba statement and Sarayya Awliya al-Dam video)
categorize.py: dedupe video posts by stripping quality suffix (جودة عالية/متوسطة/منخفضة) and matching on (time, description) — Apr 8 collapsed 9→3 video entries
Apr 8 final stats: b:2 s:6 e:12 ir:2 v:3 al:4 (was b:0 s:6 e:12 ir:1 v:9)

title: Cache-bust data JSON fetches
changes: app.js now appends ?t=Date.now() to data/YYYY-MM-DD.json fetches (matching the existing changelog.json pattern) — without this, GitHub Pages served stale JSON after a report override, so tab counts stayed frozen on the old stub values
Fix surfaced when regenerating Apr 9: JSON had b:50/s:34/e:19 but browser kept rendering 3/2/1 from the cached stub

title: Add full report for 2026-04-09
changes: Replaced stub stats for Apr 9 (b:3, s:2, e:1) with full dataset (b:50, s:34, e:19, ir:5, v:13)
Generated data/2026-04-09.json from 135 Telegram messages with 9 siren map points
Refreshed search index keywords for Apr 9 with actual targets, locations, and weapon types

title: List view: Western digits + mobile re-layout
changes: List summary 'مجموع الأحداث' total now renders in Western digits (was 'ar-EG' which produced Arabic-Indic numerals out-of-sync with the other two stats)

Mobile list rows redesigned: switched from a 3-column grid (date | pills | total) that crushed every column to a 2-row grid — date+total badge on top, pills wrap freely on a second row separated by a dashed divider
Hidden the keyword strip on mobile (it was wrapping into 2-3 lines and inflating row heights inconsistently)

title: Bayan cards: tracking column + clock strip
changes: Bayan number promoted from a faded chip to a monospace pill (#42) sitting directly under the time node — forms a twin-anchor tracking column in the right gutter
نُشر / نُفّذ timestamps moved from an orphaned footer into mini clock boxes next to the card title (dual instrument panel layout)
Fixed RTL direction bug where 'نُشر' and 'نُفّذ' labels were appearing after their values instead of before
Clock strip stacks under the title on mobile so long Arabic target names are not crushed
Other tabs (sirens, enemy, iran, videos, allies) untouched — change is opt-in via new refNum/meta config fields on createTimelineCard

title: Fix: empty bayan titles across all days
changes: Resolved 117 bayans with empty target fields across 26 report days (Mar 3 → Mar 30)
Rewrote categorize.py extract_target() with priority-ordered location patterns instead of a single regex

Handles direct-object strikes (قاعدة/ثكنة/موقع/مقرّ/مصفاة/مجمّع/شركة/مستوطنة), direction markers (باتجاه/نحو/فوق/محيط), cardinal + landmark (شرق معتقل/شمال مشروع), between-villages, air defense, multi-settlement lists, و-suffixed verbs (استهدفها/استهدفهما), and more
Preamble stripper improved to drop 'ردًّا على العدوان...' and weekday/time noise without leaving orphaned connectives
Tatweel (ـ) characters now normalized so elongated names like 'قــــاعدة عيــن شيمر' match

title: Add list view with sorting
changes: New toggle at top of homepage: 📅 Calendar | 📋 List (calendar remains default)
List view shows each report as a row with date, day name, colored stat pills, keyword preview, and total badge
Sort dropdown: date, total, bayanat, sirens, enemy, iran, videos — with asc/desc direction toggle
Summary strip: report count, total events, daily average
Whole row is clickable → opens the report
View preference saved in localStorage

title: UI: swap header buttons + clickable title
changes: Swapped positions of home button and theme toggle in report header
Home button (الرئيسية) now on the left, theme toggle (sun/moon) now on the right
Made page title 'الإعلام الحربي — التغطية الإخبارية' clickable → navigates home

title: Fix: empty bayan titles + day cutoff at 13:00
changes: Fixed extract_target regex: narrow weapon-prefix terminator list caused empty titles when bayan used بالصواريخ الموجّهة (bayan 39 on Mar 26 and others)
Extended terminators to cover بالصواريخ, بصواريخ, بمسيّرات, بسلاح, برشقة, بالمدفعية, بقذيفة, برشّاش
Broadened object-prefix strip for dual-form دبّابتَي/دبّابتين with قرب/قبالة prepositions
Fixed silent truncation: Telegram fetch limit was 200, cut busy days mid-afternoon — bumped to 2000
Re-fetched 18 truncated days (Feb 28, Mar 7/10/11/13/14/17-27, 29)
Regenerated all 40 data files — Mar 26 alone: 40→94 bayanat, 33→66 sirens, coverage 13:30→22:10
Rebuilt index.html reportStats from fresh data

title: Report: Apr 9 (escalation resumes)
changes: Added Apr 9 report: 3 bayanat, 2 sirens, 1 enemy report
Hezbollah resumes operations citing Israeli ceasefire violation
Targets: Manara settlement (rocket salvo), Tayba (Namera vehicle + Israeli force)
Fixed categorize.py shadda regex (السّاعة) for opTime + target extraction

title: Reports: Feb 28 - Mar 6
changes: Added 7 reports covering the war's opening days (Feb 28 - Mar 6)
Feb 28-Mar 1: Pre-Hezbollah strike (Iran+sirens, 181 and 154 events)
Mar 2: First Hezbollah strike, Mar 3: 13 bayanat day, Mar 5: 9 bayanat + Radwan assault
Total coverage: 40 days (Feb 28 - Apr 8)

title: Bug fixes + README
changes: Fix: reports stuck on loading screen (missing closing brace in renderReport)
Fix: weapon chart always showing 'أخرى' (stale .bayan-details selector → .weapon-chip)
Fix: empty hourly operations chart (stale .t-val selector → .node-time)
Added project README.md

title: Reports: March 7-20
changes: Added 14 historical reports: March 7-20 (1,664 total events across two weeks)
Total coverage now spans 33 days (March 7 - April 8)
Notable: Mar 11 had 74 sirens (highest single-day), Mar 12 had 23 Iran entries

title: Reports: March 21-27
changes: Added 7 historical reports: March 21-27 (788 total events across the week)
Total coverage now spans 19 days (March 21 - April 8)

title: Reports: March 28-30
changes: Added 3 historical reports: March 28 (123 events), March 29 (128 events), March 30 (103 events)
Total coverage now spans 12 days (March 28 - April 8)

title: Report: April 8
changes: Day 9 report: IRGC True Promise 4 Wave 100 (25+ strategic targets), Hezbollah political statements, 6 siren alerts (Haifa/Beer Sheva/Tel Aviv), Golani ambush at Taybeh, 3 operation videos, Iraq/Yemen/Lebanon ally statements

title: Search Deep-Link
changes: Clicking a search result scrolls to the exact card in the report
Gold border highlight on target card for 3 seconds
URL params: ?tab=&idx=&q= for direct deep-linking

title: Global Spotlight Search
changes: Press / or Ctrl+K to open search from any page
Searches across all 8+ daily reports instantly
Category filter pills (بيانات, صفارات, إعلام, إيران, فيديو, حلفاء)
Keyboard navigation: arrow keys + Enter to open result
Arabic normalization + English aliases (merkava, haifa, drone...)
Recent searches saved as suggestion chips

title: Proper Semver + Git Tags
changes: Changelog uses semver 1.0.0 through 1.0.N
Git tags created for all milestones (v1.0.0 to v1.0.41)
Cache-busting on changelog.json fetch

title: Clickable Changelog Modal
changes: Version badge in footer opens changelog modal
Changelog data stored in changelog.json
Auto-increment patch version on every git push

title: Versioning + LTR Dates
changes: Added visible version to all footers
LTR direction for calendar grid, month nav, day nav, timeline nodes
Pre-push git hook auto-increments patch number

title: Timeline Redesign (Option C v2)
changes: Unified timeline card design for all 6 tabs
Operation time in timeline nodes instead of statement numbers
Color-coded cards: purple (settlement), orange (tank), blue (deep strike)
Red hit indicator on nodes for confirmed strikes
Phase dividers show operation count
Subtle card tint matching operation type

title: Token Optimization
changes: Python categorizer script replaces LLM agent for message categorization
Report generation cost: ~230k tokens down to ~11k (97% reduction)

title: SPA Architecture
changes: Migrated from per-day HTML files to single-page app (report.html + app.js + styles.css)
JSON data files replace generated HTML (data/*.json)
One rendering engine for all reports — no more duplicated CSS/JS
Updated index.html links and report-enhancements.js for SPA URLs

title: Full Text Format Fix
changes: Fixed full text display across all reports (Apr 1-7)
JS object + DOM textContent approach preserves Arabic line breaks
Full bayan text includes bismillah, Quran verses, date, hashtags

title: Landing Page + Polish
changes: Custom domain (harbi-reports.com)
Mobile responsiveness: smaller text, tighter gaps, no overflow
Global smart search on landing page with Arabic normalization
Auto-categorization dashboards for enemy + Iran tabs

title: Maps + Enhancements
changes: Shared report-enhancements.js with auto-categorization dashboards
Hero summary, prev/next day navigation, operations map
Fullscreen button on all maps, dark/light tile swap
Siren categorization: region bars, type pills, timeline, filters

title: Interactive Features
changes: Interactive siren map with Leaflet (hover, click, popups)
Bayanat categorization dashboard
Dark/light theme toggle
Calendar landing page with heatmap and tooltips

title: Initial Release
changes: First reports: Apr 6-7, 2026
6-tab layout: bayanat, sirens, enemy media, Iran, videos, allies
Full text toggle on every card
Smart search with Arabic normalization + English aliases
